design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed = hash('Super vs Orchids|competitor_comparison|people comparing Super with Orchids|2026-07-06T00:42:25.969026+00:00') → hero_architecture=comparison-lab, typography=Cabinet Grotesk, components=[decision-matrix, workflow-playbook-timeline, source-card-dossier], gsap=[pinned-chapter-scroll, hover-physics]. AIDA Check: Hero attracts with clear contrast, Interest via market proof, Desire via workflow depth, Action via persistent CTAs. Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk confirmed (no Inter). Hero Math Verification: clamp headline 42–72px, max-width 1100px → 2–3 lines. Bento Density Verification: compare-grid uses grid-auto-flow: dense. GSAP Motion Selection: guarded GSAP with ScrollTrigger fallback. Label Sweep & Button Check: primary black pill CTA, secondary white CTA verified.
headline: Super vs Orchids — personal AI agents compared for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Orchids. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats.
site_brief.title: Super vs Orchids

summary: A fair, source‑backed comparison of Super and Orchids for people evaluating personal AI agents. Orchids explores automation concepts; Super focuses on durable computer‑use workflows with a reusable computer-use cache that improves repeated execution.
